name: American Integrity Insurance Group, Inc.

profile.description: American Integrity Insurance Group, Inc. operates as a property and casualty insurance company primarily in Florida. The company offers personal residential property insurance for single-family homeowners, condominium owners, manufactured homes, vacant dwellings, and investment properties. It also provides commercial residential, dwelling property, and specialty insurance products with optional endorsements for enhanced coverage.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): American Integrity completed its IPO in 2024 and has grown gross premiums written 48.5% YoY in Q3 2025, expanding from a Florida-focused homeowners insurer into broader specialty lines including commercial residential and manufactured home coverage [1]. The transition from private to public company and rapid premium growth represent meaningful business evolution, though the core business model remains traditional property insurance. AI Deployment (1/2): The company employs a VP of Innovations & Data (John Walsh) leading business intelligence and data analytics across the organization [2]. While this suggests data-driven underwriting and claims processing, no specific AI-powered products or quantified AI savings have been disclosed. Market Share (1/2): American Integrity is a meaningful player in Florida homeowners insurance, writing over $900M in gross premiums and growing rapidly through rate adequacy and policy count expansion [1]. However, Florida's property insurance market is highly fragmented with dozens of regional carriers. Pipeline (1/2): Revenue guidance is implicit in the strong rate environment and policy count growth, but no specific product launches or market expansion timelines have been articulated beyond continued Florida property growth. R&D Intensity (0/1): Insurance companies do not conduct traditional R&D; the company invests in data analytics and technology infrastructure but at levels typical for a mid-size insurer.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): American Integrity's core values — integrity, commitment, teamwork, humility, passion and fun — are clearly articulated and embedded in company culture, with a servant leadership philosophy where leaders focus on serving others rather than dictating instructions [2]. The name itself signals intentionality about ethical business conduct. However, the purpose does not articulate a transformative vision beyond being a good insurance company. Community Impact (1/2): The company has been recognized as a USA Today Top Workplaces USA for four consecutive years (2021-2024), a Tampa Bay Times Top Workplace for 10 years, and one of the Best Places to Work in Insurance for 11 consecutive years (2014-2024) [2]. These awards reflect genuine community and employee investment, though specific charitable programs or foundation activities are not prominently highlighted. Employee Retention (2/2): Exceptional workplace recognition — 10 consecutive years as Tampa Bay Times Top Workplace (top-3 among mid-size companies in 2023), 11 consecutive years as Best Places to Work in Insurance, and inaugural America's Best Workplaces honoree in 2024 [2]. The Friday leadership team meetings and servant leadership culture suggest strong employee engagement and retention. Insider Buying (1/2): Significant insider buying in May-June 2026 — 10% beneficial owner James Sowell purchased 136,356 shares ($2.3M), Chairman David Clark bought 5,830 shares ($100K), and Directors Mathis ($50K) and Smathers ($49K) purchased shares [3]. Net insider buying is strongly positive with no insider sales recorded.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: American Integrity's financials are exceptionally strong across every measured dimension. D/E of 9.9% reflects minimal leverage, well below the 70% target [4]. ROE of 31.2% is more than double the 15% target, demonstrating outstanding capital efficiency. Revenue growth of 26.5% YoY far exceeds the 10% target, driven by premium growth from rate adequacy and policy count expansion [1]. Two-year revenue CAGR of approximately 17.3% (2023-2025) also exceeds targets. Forward P/E of 5.6x is extraordinarily low for a growing insurer, reflecting Florida catastrophe risk discounting. Profit margin of 27.6% exceeds the 20% target. PEG ratio unavailable — excluded from calculation. All available metrics score at ceiling (1.0), producing a perfect Infrastructure score. Liquidity floor cleared: 110,369 avg volume × $16.76 = $1.85M daily [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Property and casualty insurance in Florida is protected by significant regulatory barriers — state-level licensing, rate filing requirements, statutory capital requirements, and Department of Financial Services oversight create moats that AI-native entrants cannot easily breach [1]. Physical property inspection, claims adjustment after hurricanes, and local agent relationships add layers of defensibility. However, AI is transforming underwriting, claims processing, and fraud detection across the insurance industry, and AI-native insurtech companies (Lemonade, Root, Hippo) are demonstrating that technology can compress operating costs and improve risk selection. American Integrity's core underwriting function could be partially automated, and competitors using AI for superior risk pricing could gain advantages. AI Opportunity (1/1): The company employs a VP of Innovations & Data leading business intelligence and data analytics operations [2]. While not yet generating direct AI revenue, the application of predictive analytics to underwriting, claims triage, and catastrophe modeling represents nascent AI-driven value creation within the business.

infinite_game.summary: American Integrity demonstrates genuine infinite-game qualities through its servant leadership philosophy, exceptional 10+ year workplace recognition streak, and consistent cultural values under founder-CEO Bob Ritchie's 20-year tenure. The company's focus on integrity and employee empowerment aligns with Sinek's principles. However, concentration in Florida property insurance limits strategic flexibility, and the company has not yet faced a true existential test requiring transformative reinvention.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): American Integrity's servant leadership philosophy and deeply embedded core values (integrity, commitment, teamwork, humility, passion, fun) represent a genuine purpose beyond profit [2]. CEO Bob Ritchie has maintained these values consistently since founding the company in 2006, and the 10+ consecutive years of workplace awards validate that the culture is lived, not just stated. The Friday leadership team meetings where 'there is no room for ego' suggest purpose-driven organizational behavior. However, the cause — being an ethical, people-first insurer — while genuine, is more about how they operate than a transformative vision of a better future.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): Exceptional evidence of a high-trust organization. Eleven consecutive years as Best Places to Work in Insurance, 10 consecutive years as Tampa Bay Times Top Workplace (top-3 in 2023), USA Today Top Workplaces for four straight years, and inaugural America's Best Workplaces recognition in 2024 [2]. The servant leadership model, weekly leadership team meetings focused on collaborative problem-solving, and 313-person organization with 20 years of founder leadership continuity create strong psychological safety. Multiple independent workplace awards across different evaluation methodologies consistently validate the culture.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No evidence of management referencing competitors' strengths as motivation for improvement. American Integrity competes in Florida's fragmented property insurance market against carriers like Citizens, Heritage, FedNat, and HCI Group, but management commentary focuses on rate adequacy, loss ratios, and market opportunities rather than competitive learning [1]. The company's competitive positioning language emphasizes its own cultural strengths rather than acknowledging what rivals do better.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (0/2): American Integrity has remained focused exclusively on Florida property insurance since its 2006 founding, writing homeowners, condo, manufactured home, and dwelling policies in a single state [1]. While this concentration has produced strong financial results, the company has not demonstrated strategic flexibility — no geographic expansion, no business model pivot, and no diversification beyond adjacent property lines. The company is too young and too narrowly focused to have faced or passed an existential flexibility test. A major hurricane season or Florida regulatory change would test whether the company can adapt.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): American Integrity demonstrated courage by continuing to write Florida homeowners insurance during 2022-2023 when multiple Florida carriers went insolvent (FedNat, Weston, UPC), maintaining rate discipline and underwriting standards rather than retreating from the market [1]. The company grew premiums 48.5% YoY in Q3 2025 while competitors fled, showing conviction in its underwriting model. The decision to IPO in 2024 and the leadership succession plan (Jon Ritchie as President) reflect long-term planning. However, these are rational business decisions in a favorable market, not costly bets that sacrifice near-term earnings.

investment_thesis: American Integrity is a rapidly growing Florida property insurer trading at a deeply discounted 3.5x trailing P/E and 5.6x forward P/E, with fundamentals that rival the best mid-cap insurers. Revenue grew 26.5% YoY to $276.5M in 2025, ROE is 31.2%, profit margin is 27.6%, and D/E is just 9.9% [1][4]. The company has thrived while competitors went insolvent, growing gross premiums written 48.5% in Q3 2025 amid Florida's hardening property market. Significant insider buying — $2.5M in purchases by a 10% owner and multiple directors in May-June 2026 — signals leadership conviction [3]. The servant leadership culture, validated by 11 consecutive years of workplace awards, creates a durable organizational advantage in employee retention and claims service quality [2]. At current prices, the market is pricing in catastrophic hurricane risk that may not materialize.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on catastrophe concentration and Florida regulatory risk. American Integrity writes exclusively in Florida, one of the most hurricane-exposed states, meaning a single Category 4+ storm hitting Tampa Bay could generate losses exceeding the company's equity base [1]. The company's $328M market cap makes it vulnerable to catastrophic events that larger diversified insurers can absorb. Earnings growth of -63.4% YoY reflects the lumpy nature of insurance earnings, where a single storm can wipe out multiple years of profit. Florida's property insurance market is politically volatile — legislative changes to litigation rules, building codes, or reinsurance requirements could alter the competitive landscape unpredictably. The loss ratio deteriorated from 30.9% to 37.3% in Q1 2026, suggesting potential adverse development [1]. Geographic concentration means American Integrity cannot diversify away from Florida hurricane risk, and climate change may increase storm frequency and severity over time.
key_catalysts: Continued premium growth in Florida's hardening property market where competitor insolvencies have created policy acquisition opportunities — gross premiums written grew 48.5% YoY in Q3 2025 [1]
Significant insider buying totaling $2.5M+ in May-June 2026 by a 10% beneficial owner and multiple board members signals institutional conviction in the stock's undervaluation [3]
Potential geographic expansion beyond Florida or product line diversification could unlock multiple expansion from the current 3.5x P/E catastrophe-discounted valuation [1]
key_risks: Single-state concentration in Florida creates existential hurricane risk — a direct Category 4+ hit on Tampa Bay or Miami metro could generate losses exceeding company equity and threaten solvency [1]
Loss ratio deterioration from 30.9% to 37.3% in Q1 2026 may signal adverse claims development or inadequate reserves, compressing the 27.6% profit margin [1]

Florida's politically volatile insurance regulatory environment could change litigation rules, rate-setting authority, or reinsurance requirements, altering the competitive dynamics that currently favor rate-adequate carriers [1]
sources: American Integrity Insurance Group Q1 2026 and Full Year 2025 Earnings Releases — $276.5M revenue, 26.5% growth, Q3 2025 gross premiums +48.5%, loss ratio data, SEC 8-K filings
